title: Api Access
text:
---
title: API Access
description: API access to NRP-managed LLMs via the Envoy AI Gateway
---
import { Card, Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

To access NRP LLMs through the [Envoy AI Gateway](https://aigateway.envoyproxy.io), you need to be a [member of a group with the LLM flag](/documentation/userdocs/start/hierarchy). Your membership info is on the [namespaces page](/namespaces).

Start by [creating a token](/llmtoken). The token authenticates against an OpenAI-compatible endpoint:

<Card title="Envoy AI API endpoint">
`https://ellm.nrp-nautilus.io/v1`
</Card>

You can use this endpoint with `curl` or any OpenAI-compatible client.

```bash
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <your_token>" https://ellm.nrp-nautilus.io/v1/models
```

<Aside type="caution" title="About Max Output Tokens">
Only specify `max_tokens` / `max_output_tokens` / "Max Output Tokens" when you understand what they mean. This is the **maximum output length** under the context length, not the total context length. If your client requires a value, set it to roughly 1/3–1/4 of the context window. **Never specify the full context length here — the model will error.**
</Aside>

## Python

```python title="nrp-llm.py"
import os
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(
    api_key=os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY"),
    base_url="https://ellm.nrp-nautilus.io/v1",
)

completion = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-oss",
    messages=[
        {"role": "system", "content": "Talk like a pirate."},
        {"role": "user", "content": "How do I check if a Python object is an instance of a class?"},
    ],
)

print(completion.choices[0].message.content)
```

## Bash + curl

List available models:

```bash
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>" https://ellm.nrp-nautilus.io/v1/models
```

Send a chat completion:
